agent_approach_summary: The agent went down an unrelated path, investigating and editing MailViewer/MailViewerViewModel lifecycle, rendering, and offline-spinner behavior. It never focused on the stated SendMailModel API/test simplification from Promise.resolve(new Map()) to new Map().
reasoning: This task appears clean. The problem statement explicitly says SendMailModel tests are unnecessarily wrapping Map objects in Promise.resolve() when calling initWithDraft, and the F2P test patch directly reflects that by changing two test calls from Promise.resolve(new Map()) to new Map(). A reasonable, spec-derived fix is to update the relevant SendMailModel/MailEditor signatures and implementation from Promise<InlineImages> to InlineImages for these code paths, removing the await/Promise requirement. That is exactly what the relevant part of the gold patch does. The large MailViewer/MailView portions of the gold patch are unrelated overpatch noise, not what the tests require. The agent failed because it misunderstood the task and spent its effort making speculative MailViewer changes unrelated to the test diff; it never addressed the SendMailModel contract change the tests actually exercise. There is no sign that the tests require hidden internals, exact magic strings, or an undiscoverable behavior beyond the problem statement.
